title: Bridge Essay: Seeing Jesus in toast: neural and behavioral correlates of face pareidolia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Seeing, Jesus, in, toast:, neural.

**Seeing Jesus in toast: neural and behavioral correlates of face pareidolia.** — Cortex. PMID: 24583223. DOI: 10.1016/j.cortex.2014.01.013. Face pareidolia is the illusory perception of non-existent faces. The present study, for the first time, contrasted behavioral and neural responses of face pareidolia with those of letter pareidolia to explore face-specific behavioral and neural responses during illusory face processing. Participants were shown pure-noise images but were led to believe that 50% of them contained either faces or letters; they reported seeing faces or letters illusorily 34% and 38% of the time, respectively.
